title: RPi Explained: The Responsible Person for Import
author: B. Subramanian
excerpt: A UK QP explains the responsible person import (RPi): what the role does, how it differs from the QP and RP, and how to resource it for MHRA compliance.
cover_image_prompt: A photorealistic clinical-luxury banner of a pharmaceutical quality professional's gloved hands reviewing imported-batch certification paperwork beside a sleek tablet showing a supply-chain audit trail, set on a polished deep navy logistics-and-laboratory bench; cool teal and cyan tones dominate, with a single subtle green accent from a status indicator light, soft natural window light falling from the left, shallow depth of field gently blurring sealed medicine cartons and a barcode scanner in the background, pristine, authoritative and trustworthy atmosphere, 4:3 aspect ratio, no text, no logos.

faqs.q: When is a Responsible Person (import) actually required?
faqs.a: An RPi is required on a Wholesale Dealer's Authorisation when a UK company imports certain medicines into Great Britain from countries on the MHRA's approved list. The role confirms that those products were certified by a QP in the exporting country to standards equivalent to UK GMP before GB release. If you only distribute domestically or import from a site already covered by a UK MIA and QP, an RPi may not be needed, so the activity on your licence determines the requirement.

faqs.q: Does the RPi re-certify imported batches like a QP?
faqs.a: No. The RPi does not repeat QP certification or re-test the product. Instead, the named individual confirms, on the basis of documented evidence, that a QP in the country of export has already certified the batch to equivalent standards and that the supporting records are complete and trustworthy. It is a verification and confirmation step, not a second certification.

faqs.q: Can the RPi and RP be the same person?
faqs.a: They can, provided that one individual holds the competence and capacity to discharge both sets of duties and is named accordingly on the Wholesale Dealer's Authorisation. Many importers do combine the roles, but the RPi function carries the specific responsibility of confirming equivalent QP certification of imported products. The key is that the MHRA is satisfied the named person is genuinely available and able to perform every duty assigned to them.
